data.title: Optical Character Recognition for Early Handwriting Legibility Assessment
data.authors: Franceli L Cibrian, Kayla Anderson, Yingchen ‘Yuki’ Chen, Lauren Min, Lizbeth Escobedo
data.description: Monitoring children’s handwriting, such as avoiding writing assignments, displaying uneven letter formation, or showing slow writing speed, can help identify developmental and academic issues early. Poor handwriting affects up to 34% of children, leading to academic and self-esteem challenges. Handwriting assessments, typically conducted by teachers, are often delayed due to workload and could be subjective and inconsistent. This paper explores the potential of Optical Character Recognition (OCR) technology to augment and ease handwriting assessments. Based on an evaluation of 10 OCR algorithms using 33 handwriting samples assessed by two experts, the research indicates that Pen to Print and Google are the most accurate for character recognition. However, Handwriting to Text Converter and Smart Text Recognizer align more closely with expert evaluations. These findings suggest that the latter …
